review_title: Great for Apple Device Management
description: Deployment and setup was very quick and easy. We only use for iPads and Apple TV's and I am easily able to manage there without any major issues.
author: Rachel H.
pros: Exceptional ease of use. I think the interface is very user friendly. Device enrollment, app deployment and updates is very simple. If I encounter an issue, the customer service is also very exceptional.
cons: Really the only two dislikes I have is the apps not updating with me forcing the update and the price. We are not a large company with under 300 iPads/Apple TV's deployed, but the price is a bit much. The cost/benefit ration is not there.

review_title: Apple hardware management made easy
description: Jamf Made Ipad/macbook management simple for deployment to classes and installing volume licenses. It also made tracking devices easy. we could locate ones still in the building or find ones that were stolen
author: Curt C.
pros: I could make an install package based on what has changed. this means I could install apps, change settings, add or remove files and make a deplorable package file easy
cons: We only used it for apple products. We had to use other product for windows and chromebooks. This isn't really a problem with Jamf, it just that we had to use several MDM suites

review_title: IT & Process Manager
description: Waste of time and money, get Iru/Kandji instead if you want to have a clean and easy setup without tons of scripts and unnecessary workarounds. Their migration tool is great.
author: Vincent S.
pros: I didn't really like it because standard functions that every company needs where a total pain to setup and the documentation is behind a paywall aka courses.
cons: Standard functions like auto-updates, self-service, auto-download apps when an employee opens the laptop for the first time, the dock configuration needing an extra script, ALL needed a bunch of scripts, workarounds and it's just a waste of time. Customer support needs ages to answer and if they do they refer you to some random article.

review_title: Good MDM Option
description: Overall it has been positive. It is a good service for us, and it has worked well. I was thrown into a project with having to use Jamf Pro and having zero knowledge of it. I was able to quickly pick it up and become successful with it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Jamf Pro is easy to use and easy to implement. Their Customer Support is great. They have great Device Management, and seem to be a good value for money.
cons: A bit of a learning curve at first. But after you get the basics down, it is easy to get familiar with all the other parts of it.

review_title: Jamf makes Apple device management easy
description: Jamf is superb value for money and probably the best MDM solution we have tried over the years.
author: Manny B.
pros: Jamf has enabled us to manage out fleet of staff and student Macs with ease, offering super support for day-1 features every year, and smoothing out all sorts of device restriction/interaction scenarios, along with app deployment
cons: The interface was a little clunky at the start, and we had some teething issues with device connectivity, but these issues have all gone away in recent times.

review_title: MSP Managers Honest Review
description: Coming from Meraki MDM Jamf was a totally different experience. Its hard to use, complicated to understand and requires a lot of background in development to understand.
author: Harrison K.
pros: I like how easy it is to onboard a new computer through JAMF. We use HTML onboarding and with the click of a button a clients computer/tablet/phone is onboarded.
cons: I dislike the interface, navigating the interface, and honestly finding anything. It makes it almost impossible to be an admin of the platform without a certification or some background in dev work.

review_title: Excellent Apple-centric MDM
description: Overall, Jamf Pro is a great tool to manage Apple devices. There are a few zones where it could be improved and the team are hopefully working on them. Their support team is, however, always fast to answer and they provide great help.
author: Michaël D.
pros: Jamf Pro is a powerful tool when it comes to manage Apple devices, whether they are laptops of mobile phones/tablets. It offers all sorts of controls and streamlines deployment of Configuration Profiles or Policies. Their API, though a bit cumbersome as it's divided in two versions (Legacy and Pro) allows for automations of almost all aspects of the platform.
cons: Some part of the interface are a bit dated and lacks flexibility. Feature requests around these issues are not always acknowledged despite them being sometimes opened for years.

review_title: Pretty good but takes time to get used to
description: I would say overall quality is good but not amazing. It does what it should most of the time, but sometimes things feel slower or more complicated than needed.
author: Benjamin T.
pros: really strong when it comes to managing apple devices, thats probably the best part. once you get used to it you can automate a lot and save time. also pretty flexible, you can customize a lot of things how you need it. overall it works reliable most of the time
cons: the interface is kinda confusing sometimes and not very intuitive. also pretty expensive for what it is. support can be slow at times which is a bit annoying. some features feel more complicated than they should be and take time to figure out

review_title: Best in class for Apple Device Management
author: Annie G.
pros: We use Jamf at our company to help manage all of our Apple devices, specifically Macbooks. It helps our IT team streamline the laptop software and setting updates across all laptops.
cons: Jamf is specific to Apple products only so you will need to find another solution if you have windows/PC.

review_title: Good once set up
description: Not quite as simple as expected, but still a good product for the end user once initiated. Ultimately the value for money will depend on use. For us, a better solution would have been a more centralised approach.
author: Jon J.
pros: Once setup it gives a clear visual overview of your devices and it is easy to push out updates, and permissions.
cons: Some of the setup and integration was a bit tricky. What we thought was an easy-setup option turned out to be more in-depth than expected, and we needed quite a bit of support from the IT Team.

review_title: Jamf Pro makes Apple device management easy.
author: Mathieu R.
pros: Jamf Pro handled device management smoothly, especially for Macs. Deployments, updates, and security policies felt effortless, and it saved a lot of time managing development machines.
cons: Some actions take a few extra clicks compared to what I expected. The learning curve can be noticeable in the first days, but it becomes intuitive once the structure clicks.

review_title: Highly recommend!
author: Michelle S.
pros: The UI is very intuitive and clean. It is organized in a way that makes sense for any user and makes management of all the employees and their devices very simple. It has made the onboarding process so much easier as well as a better more clear control of security on hardware.
cons: There is nothing that I can think of. It has all and more for possible integrations with all the applications we use.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Good Apple device management tool
author: Virginia B.
pros: Being specifically tailored for Apple devices, it ensures a full compatibility with these devices and optimal functionality. It is complete and offers scalability and automation features, making the human job quicker and easier. It has a user-friendly interface, allowing to understand quickly what is the status of the device environment.
cons: Its initial setup management is quite time and resource consuming, but it is normal if you adopt it in a large environment. Being Apple centric, on the other hand, even if it allows this full compatibilty makes it not always adoptable in any company, which may have not only apple devices in its environment.

review_title: Legendary #1 Mobile Device Management Suite in the globe
description: Innovative platform for device management.Easy to implement and deploy.Hassle free in authenticating users and devices.Excellent solution for device inventory management.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Device management ,Single sign on, Patch manament,Inventory management,Authentication management
cons: User interface feature should be improved.Approving device should be improved.

review_title: Great for end users but can be a processor hog
author: Elizabeth K.
pros: It makes getting remote assistance very easy for me as an end user
cons: Seems to run in the background a lot and use a lot of CPU time, especially in conjunction with Chrome; sometimes my (not new) MacBook heats up from it.

review_title: JAMF is essential for Device Management
description: JAMF makes is so easy to manage our Apple devices and keep them secure and compliant. This really helps us ensure Apple devices can easily integrate in our Microsoft Active Directory Domain.The integration with third party vendors really makes it a pleasure to use this.
author: Louwrens N.
pros: The overall ease of use makes JAMF really pleasant to work with. We can deploy configurations and compliance policies very easily and this ensures our Apple devices are always secure and compliant.
cons: At this point we do not think there is anything JAMF can improve on, they do already have so many great features.

review_title: Jamf Pro from an Admin's Point of view
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Ease of use Perfect integration with Apple Timely Updates
cons: I would like to be able to put devices into specific buckets like AirWatch

review_title: Jamf @ UCLA Library
description: Our Macs were largely unmanaged before Jamf, and now we have a good handle on everything and are able to provide a level of support that was impossible before. It's allowed us to not worry (as much) about what device people choose to use for work. It's also allowed flexibility when purchasing new devices for different purposes. If it's made by Apple, we know we can handle it.
author: Jonathanwilson W.
pros: The completeness of features - just about anything you can do with an Apple device, you can do with Jamf. I don't have to worry about not being able to do something, including on the day new software is released.
cons: The learning curve is high - I would be struggling if I hadn't taken the Jamf training courses (200 and 300). I struggle to explain concepts to employees that haven't taken the training, even when they've taken the Jamf 100. Sort of related is the support for legacy features/systems - it can be hard to tell what is relevant, such as when you type 'sudo jamf help' - there is a lot there that you'd never use.

review_title: Fantastic MDM for Apple OS and IOS Devices
description: Ease of use, customization and customer support are the best. This product was designed for Apple products and that is where is shines.
author: Chris S.
pros: What I love most about Jamf is the ease of use. The first couple of iterations while workable, did not do what we needed in the education field. What was the saving grace, was the customer support. They came up with work arounds for us until IOS 10 hit. This makes adding software to our district IPads a snap as well as policies for the student use. As for support, I ALWAYS get someone who contacts me within a couple of hours of submitting a ticket.
cons: My main problem is that there is no find my device or location tracking as with other MDM's. We have thousands of student IPads and a lot of disappearing on a daily basis. This would be a great feature to have.

review_title: jamF the only way to go with a Mac device
description: Two thumbs and toes up 100%, love this product and cannot wait to learn more as I enhance my training classes to learn more
author: timothy L.
pros: Ease of use and management of devices as well as computers. Configuration roll outs, software roll outs, self service options, inventory management, report management and much more
cons: lots of overhead and training is necessary but as you get more training these cons disappear before your eys.

review_title: Pretty good management system for Macs. Don't even consider LANDesk.
description: Because it is a MDM solution that actually works, with reliable uptime and easy maintenance requirements, I am the hero for having pushed for JAMFPro.
author: Mike B.
pros: Fairly easy to figure out what it takes to make it work if you are of average intelligence for this sort of thing. In my case, it was difficult, until I figured it out. Patch management was the priority for us and it turns you have to be a little creative in trying to get the information extracted from the Macs (depending on the application) in order to determine if it needs to be patched. In another words, it's not as turnkey as much as I was led to believe. Support at JAMFNation is fantastic. Those folks have tackled most of the problems and that's where I located the scripts and other nuances required to make it work. No other resource like it. Newbies and super rockstars all contribute to make things work. JAMF staff also helpful. Make sure you contribute too!
cons: For me personally, the required Jumpstart training you need to purchase didn't work so well. Did not know enough about what I was doing to ask intelligent questions. After the training, fighting the learning curve began. There are no step-by-step procedures (or friendly user manual) to help a newbie get the system up and running. Mind you I say this as a complete novice and not a very smart one at that.

review_title: The premiere Apple device management solution
description: As my review title says, Jamf pro is the premiere application for managing Apple devices, second to none. The support and community alone put it above the competition in my opinion.
author: Sam N.
pros: The builtin capabilities of Jamf Pro, combined with the flexible API and strong community make it the ideal tool for managing Apple devices. The support is top notch, and they are always adding value to the product.
cons: I have not encountered any issues that Jamf support hasn't been able to resolve.

review_title: JAMF Pro Review
author: Taylor M.
pros: Integration is very simple. Knowledge base support and customer support.
cons: Pricing and small learning curve. Challenging in a small start.

review_title: Electronic Management Made Easy
description: Jamf has allowed my team and me to raise our level of customer assistance and machine management. Since we first integrated it, it has made quick ease of processes and asset management.
author: Edgar R.
pros: I like how it allows us to view which machines are in need of updates, what updates crashed, as well as the ability to manage machine inventory. All these features and a clean interface make it easy to navigate.
cons: Although this may be centralized to a few issues, at times the synchronization seems to be delayed compared to ServiceNow.

review_title: The best for Apple device management
description: It is the best out there for Apple MDMs. That said, the field is still pretty bad. Not all of the issues are with Jamf, as Apple has certainly bungled a few things, like Apple School Manager. But if you're committed to the Apple way of life, Jamf is absolutely the only way to go, and has certainly made things easier on me since we switched from a competitor.
author: Jax W.
pros: It does everything you need it to in terms of managing whatever you buy from Apple, with a focus on as much automation for mass deployment as possible. Since they focus on Apple, they have all the latest features available as soon as possible.
cons: When things break, they can break badly, and a lot of solutions involve running command line scripts and requesting service restarts from support, so if you aren't willing to dig into these sorts of things, it might be difficult for you when support gives you this type of solution. Also, their marketing can be very deceptive, with their analysis on costs phrased as to make apples-to-oranges comparisons and obfuscate their competition's benefits.
